name: Emily Mason
focus: Planetary atmospheres • Mars boundary layer processes • Meteorological data analysis
bio: Emily L. Mason is a planetary scientist and postdoctoral researcher at Northern Arizona University, working within the Planetary Instrumentation eXperimentation and Exploration Laboratory (PIXEL). Her research focuses on planetary atmospheres and boundary layer processes, with a particular emphasis on Mars. She specializes in analyzing meteorological data from both surface missions and orbital observations, using tools such as infrared spectroscopy and atmospheric modeling to study dust lifting, temperature variability, and atmospheric dynamics. Her work includes contributions to the Mars Science Laboratory (Curiosity Rover), where she investigates how surface conditions interact with atmospheric behavior, including dust devils and seasonal changes. Dr. Mason integrates data analysis, remote sensing, and modeling to better understand planetary climates, helping to improve interpretations of environmental processes on Mars and other planetary bodies.
